title: corydalis caseana subsp. aquae-gelidae
intro: Corydalis caseana subsp. aquae-gelidae, also known as the Water-loving Corydalis, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the poppy family, Papaveraceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. The medicinal properties of this plant are attributed to its ability to inhibit the production of pain-causing chemicals in the body, offering relief from conditions such as arthritis, muscle pain, and menstrual cramps. Corydalis caseana subsp. aquae-gelidae can be prepared as an herbal tincture, capsule, or tea, allowing for various forms of ingestion to harness its medicinal benefits.
